repository_change.change: removed default --cov-fail-under=18 from pytest addopts and replaced it with a comment stating CI enforces the threshold explicitly for full-suite runs

requested_suite_presence.resolution: all currently exercised suites remain present; this pass validated that the targeted local coverage-threshold fix makes narrow reruns interpretable while CI still enforces the canonical 18 percent gate

bootstrap_attempts.resolution: local API bootstrap failed because the active runtime was missing jwt
subsequent local API bootstrap failed because the active runtime was missing sarif-om support needed by normalizers
later local API bootstrap failed because the active runtime was missing structlog

notes: this pass did not produce a new preserved self-scan log under data/autonomous-reports
the newest existing machine-readable autonomous report before this refresh remained data/autonomous-reports/autonomous-foundation-report-20260408T032717Z.json
the primary accomplishment of this pass was validation-harness correctness rather than a fresh end-to-end self-scan baseline

validation_runs.name: combined_confirmation_rerun
notes: this is the decisive confirmation artifact for this cycle because it clears the unchanged 18 percent coverage gate after the pytest configuration fix
it shows the previously exercised high-visibility and broader suites can pass together without weakening CI policy

validation_runs.name: high_visibility_confirmation
notes: the branding, BN/LR, and AI-consensus path is functionally green again
its low isolated coverage is expected because the selected slice is intentionally narrow

validation_runs.name: broader_impacted_rerun
notes: the app-factory and overlay/configuration path is functionally green after the configuration fix
this isolated slice still sits below the full-repository denominator, which is why CI remains the authoritative coverage gate

environment_repairs.summary: updated pyproject.toml so generic local pytest runs no longer inherit the repository-wide coverage fail-under threshold by default

code_changes.summary: removed default --cov-fail-under=18 from pytest addopts and documented that CI enforces the threshold explicitly for full-suite runs
updated the branch status narrative with the actual configuration fix, current confirmation evidence, and remaining risks
captured this cycle's pytest configuration fix, confirmation reruns, bootstrap evidence, and recommended next actions

remaining_risks: future autonomous cycles can still lose time to runtime bootstrap gaps such as missing jwt, sarif-om, or structlog before validation even starts
this pass did not produce a fresh durable self-scan log, so the latest structured backlog counts still come from the earlier 2026-04-08 report
isolated narrow slices still show low coverage percentages because they do not represent the full repository denominator, even when functionally green
the existing self-scan backlog described by the prior structured report remains materially present until a fresh scan and remediation pass are completed

recommended_next_actions.rationale: this pass produced a real repository change and fresh confirmation evidence that targeted local validation is now more truthful while CI policy remains intact

recommended_next_actions.action: add a lightweight runtime preflight for jwt, sarif_om, structlog, and sklearn before long autonomous cycles begin
recommended_next_actions.rationale: the preserved bootstrap logs show repeated import-level failures that are cheap to detect before starting expensive validation work

recommended_next_actions.rationale: this cycle improved validation-harness correctness but did not preserve a new self-scan artifact under data/autonomous-reports
